message:
refactor(api,conformance,dispatch): code quality cleanup — remove unused import, suppress dead_code warnings, use let-chains

Three small quality improvements across different crates:

- fj-api/src/lib.rs: Remove unused `smallvec` import from the test module.
  The test that previously used `smallvec![...]` was already updated to use
  `vec![...].into()` (consistent with the rest of the codebase), but the
  import was left behind. This eliminates a compiler warning.

- fj-conformance/tests/control_flow_conformance.rs: Add #[allow(dead_code)]
  to `cond_add_mul_jaxpr()` and `nested_cond_jaxpr()` test helper functions.
  These construct valid Jaxpr programs used as reference fixtures but are not
  currently called from any #[test] function, triggering dead_code warnings.
  Suppressing rather than deleting because they serve as documented examples
  of cond-based control flow Jaxpr construction.

- fj-dispatch/src/batching.rs: Refactor the nested `if` in batch_gather's
  fast-path from `if a { if let Some(b) = ... { ... } }` to a single
  `if a && let Some(b) = ... { ... }` using Rust let-chains (RFC 2497).
  This flattens one indentation level and makes the combined guard condition
  more readable, with no behavioral change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
